readings.title: Saturday Marian Office
readings.summary: The Roman tradition (codified by Alcuin of York in the 8th c., though older in liturgical practice) dedicates every Saturday in Ordinary Time that is not impeded by a higher rank to the Memoria Sanctae Mariae in Sabbato (Marian Saturday Memorial).

supplementary.title: Common of the Blessed Virgin Mary
supplementary.summary: The Common provides readings for Marian feasts that lack their own proper Second Reading, and for the Saturday Marian commemoration in Ordinary Time. The most frequently chosen readings in the modern Office include:

supplementary.title: 1962 Breviary Texts Retained for the Older Office
supplementary.summary: The pre-Vatican II Roman Breviary (the Breviarium Romanum of 1962, still in use in the Extraordinary Form) preserves additional Marian patristic texts not retained in the post-1969 universal Office.

supplementary.title: A Note on the Office of Readings as Catechesis
supplementary.summary: The patristic and doctoral readings in the Marian Office of Readings constitute, taken as a whole, a complete patristic Marian catechesis delivered to anyone who prays the Office regularly. The faithful Catholic who prays the Office across a calendar year receives:
